item.title: A question of morals and ethics
item.dc:creator: psuedon

item.title: Daughter came too and wore a "Free Sis Hugs" tee. But she was more interested in the bounce house and food.
item.description: Might be time to retire the free hugs gear! I know there are still shitty and intolerant parents out there but their kids are in better hands with a much bigger and inclusive community. And with the younger generations being brought up in more accepting homes they don't need to run into the arms of strangers once a year at Pride. I've gladly given dad hugs every year since my daughter was born and even though I felt a little sad not getting many today, I reckon Pittsburgh is doing pretty well by way of their LGBTQ family members. Doesn't mean I'm not willing to share an embrace and a good cry if someone needs it, even if it's not June! Even if they're not gay! Lol.
item.dc:creator: MyWifesMohawk

item.dc:creator: Alivewiththwgloryoflove

item.title: My friendly friend died today. He was a good dog.
item.description:
RIP Grendel. Easter, 2015, Cottonport, Louisiana to June 1, 2025, in South Carolina. His grave is about 2 1/2 feet on a side, and 4 feet deep. It is the best I could do for him.
A picture from Christmas, 2024. I can see that he aged a lot in the last six months. I don't think I'll get another dog. I'm of the age that I don't want to bury another, and I don't want to leave one behind.
item.dc:creator: CEvonK

item.dc:creator: Odoyyoxigoyxc

item.title: My Synagogue’s Booth for Ferndale, MI Pride. LGBT+ Loving, Pro Palestine, Justice Forward!
item.description: I’m so proud of how my community showed up, and we had a blast doing it. We helped raise money for food aid in Gaza and spread the word that we are PROUDLY the queerest shul in metro Detroit! Front page edit: thanks all!!! It was a great day and we chatted with more incredible FANTASTICLY sparkly, beautiful people than I could count. Except one dude in an IDF t-shirt who did several scowly walk-bys - but he can go pound sand. Hope you enjoy your local Pride events!
item.dc:creator: bunniestbunny

item.dc:creator: MadameRainbowPants

item.title: Why we need Pride
item.description: We need to celebrate Pride Month so people like Edward Ryan can live their lives free of shame or discrimination. Edward Ryan was a Colonel in the Army and a firefighter in his local community. He spent 25 years with his partner Paul and during this time he never felt he could live his life openly. He only revealed himself to the world in his obituary. “I was afraid of being ostracized: by Family, Friends, and Co-Workers. Seeing how people like me were treated, I just could not do it. Now that my secret is known, I'll forever Rest in Peace." I’m celebrating Pride Month so people like Edward can live their lives as the people they are without fear or discrimination. https://www.legacy.com/us/obituaries/timesunion-albany/name/edward-ryan-obituary?id=55294097&utm_source=webshareapi&utm_medium=share_button&utm_campaign=wsapimobile_beta
item.dc:creator: bourbonandbaddecisions

item.title: Deeper and deeper...
item.description: ..and deeper and deeper. Let this be a turning point that will doom Putin's sad and weak dictatorship into collapsing. The ruzzians had a chance to peace in their own terms but now they are losing their edge really hard and will need to back down with their demands. June 1st will go down in history.

ono
Happy pride month.
Oh no.
owo
:D
Happy pride Month Mortals.
The Republicans don't care about you and want you to die, what they DON'T realise I think is that having a load of sick and injured people with no source of aid leads to economic problems.

item.title: What Happened Today #122
item.description: ? Reality isn't just bending; our dystopia is now undeniable. Trump shares a post that Biden was "executed and replaced by clones" while Ernst jokes about constituents dying from Medicaid cuts, and somehow a terrorist with a flamethrower feels like just another Tuesday. This isn't political theater anymore. It's the systematic destruction of any shared understanding of what's real or acceptable. Meanwhile, don't rely on the algorithm. Let me send this free and private summary to you every night so that you never miss what's going on. Add your email on the homepage of GovBrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist 1. https://go.govbrief.today/senators-warn-on-russia 2. https://go.govbrief.today/china-blasts-us 3. https://go.govbrief.today/lutnick-tariffs-staying 4. https://go.govbrief.today/hegseth-australia-spending 5. https://go.govbrief.today/hernandez-golden-twice 6. https://go.govbrief.today/colorado-fire-attack 7. https://go.govbrief.today/sanctuary-cities-list-removed 8. https://go.govbrief.today/ernst-doubles-down 9. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-unhinged-robot Yesterday - https://go.govbrief.today/palantir-us-database Fighting Back - https://go.govbrief.today/shattuck-everyday-resistance
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.dc:creator: pantsforever

item.title: Stellar SkyView: oceanwide.
item.description: Light pollution is a mainstay in most cities on the Planet. Sometimes I can see a few stars here and there, but usually they can be counted on my fingers. I'm looking up at the night sky right now, and there isn't a single visible star. But check out the ocean sky, out there, in the type of darkness that illuminates.
item.dc:creator: ArcadesCinza

item.title: Sheep rescue
item.description: Thanks to Edgar's Mission: https://edgarsmission.org.au/ Q: What did sheep do before people started shaving them? A: Today's farm sheep come from generations of selective breeding to increase wool production. If we didn't breed them to grow wool so quickly they wouldn't need to be shaved by humans.
item.dc:creator: rhinokitty
